medications.drug_name: IM ketorolac / high-dose NSAID as a remote substitute for joint assessment
medications.indication_rationale: Injections cannot be given via telehealth and anti-inflammatories must not substitute for assessing/aspirating a warm effused joint; a GP can prescribe an NSAID after the joint is assessed.
contraindications_in_this_case: Warm effused joint not yet aspirated

medications.indication_rationale: Interim analgesia within limits while arranging urgent assessment.
Ease gritty conjunctivitis while safety-netting for uveitis; not a treatment for uveitis.
Do not give blind antibiotics; STI/joint results guide any antimicrobial therapy (reactive arthritis is sterile).
contraindications_in_this_case: Diagnosis not confirmed; sterile process likely

allied_health_referrals.rationale: Rheumatology for reactive arthritis; ophthalmology if uveitis features; sexual-health service for STI screening.

integrative_alternative_therapies.therapy_name: Rest/offloading and elevation of the ankle, lubricating drops
integrative_alternative_therapies.rationale: Comfort measures while awaiting assessment; not a substitute for aspiration.
Not indicated as remote flare therapy; targeted antibiotics only if STI screening confirms infection.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Watch for the ankle becoming fiery red/tense/unbearably hot or a high fever with rigors, and for any sudden eye pain, light sensitivity or vision change, seeking emergency care if they occur; rest/elevate the ankle.
Arrange STI screening and attend the in-person joint assessment; use lubricating drops for the gritty eyes.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Excludes concurrent STI/DGI and manages the reactive features.

patient_education_points.point: After a gut infection, your immune system can trigger a 'reactive' arthritis with joint, eye and waterworks inflammation — that fits your picture.

But a warm, swollen ankle has to be checked in person and often drained with a needle to be sure it isn't a joint infection (including one from an STI) — that can't be done over a chat, so you need an urgent same-day assessment, plus STI screening.
Rest and elevate the ankle and use simple painkillers within limits for now; strong anti-inflammatories aren't a substitute for getting the joint assessed.
Go to ED / an emergency eye service if the ankle becomes fiery red and unbearably hot, you get a high fever with shaking chills, or your eye becomes painful/light-sensitive or your vision blurs.

follow_up_plan.interval: Urgent same-day in-person joint assessment (± aspiration) and STI screening; rheumatology/ophthalmology as indicated
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Joint aspiration/STI results, inflammatory markers, eye status, reactive-arthritis management
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: A fiery/tense hot ankle, high fever/rigors, or sudden eye pain/vision change — go to ED / emergency eye service.

must_include_items: Arrange urgent same-day in-person joint assessment with ankle aspiration to exclude septic/gonococcal arthritis
Arrange STI screening (gonorrhoea/chlamydia) for the urethritis
Safety-net acute anterior uveitis (sudden eye pain/photophobia/vision change) and septic arthritis
Do NOT substitute remote NSAIDs/injections or blind antibiotics for assessment
acceptable_alternatives: Interim paracetamol/rest/elevation and lubricating eye drops
Direct ED if any severe septic/eye feature is present
errors_of_commission: Remote IM NSAID/high-dose NSAID as substitute for assessment
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires (1) urgent in-person joint assessment with aspiration, (2) STI screening, and (3) septic-arthritis + uveitis safety-netting. Managing a hot joint remotely is a fail.
